playbook.intent: Two weeks of work → long-form IH post that drives qualified clicks back.
playbook.scope: Bi-weekly long-form post on Indie Hackers: progress, numbers, lessons, and an explicit ask — driving qualified clicks back to the product, newsletter, or audience handle.

success-criteria.criterion: Includes numbers, lessons, and a single ask
Documented clicks to landing or newsletter

stage.name: Mine
stage.intent: Pick the most genuine story from the last 2 weeks.

tasks.task: Pick the one with a clear lesson
Note numbers that prove the story

stage.decision-gate: Advance once the lesson is one sentence.

stage.name: Draft
stage.intent: Long-form post that mixes story, numbers, and ask.

tasks.task: Open with a confession or contrarian hook

stage.decision-gate: Advance only once another indie hacker would skim and react.

stage.name: Ship
stage.intent: Post and engage to convert comments to clicks.

tasks.task: Reply to every comment in the first 12 hours
Track click-throughs to landing or newsletter

stage.decision-gate: Done once comments are handled and clicks logged.

extra.value: [Cross-posting without re-write — IH is a unique surface, Promo-only posts without a story, Daily IH posting — bi-weekly is the cadence]
[Active IH account with bio aligned to current product, Two weeks of build progress to talk about]
[Treating the playbook as a checklist instead of a decision tree, Skipping decision gates when intermediate progress feels good, Letting tools or platforms replace the founder's own judgement]

['Did each stage produce its named output or get explicitly skipped with a note?', 'Were decision gates respected or quietly bypassed?', 'Is the evidence trail strong enough to defend the verdict externally?']

{human: faion get-content indie-hackers-build-update-post --format md, agent: faion get-content indie-hackers-build-update-post --format context, json: faion get-content indie-hackers-build-update-post --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft from brainstorm-2026-05-17, persona P2 (indie hacker)'}]
